text.title: Data Pipeline Design Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Data Pipeline Design` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST partition data into bronze (raw fidelity) → silver (schema + dedup) → gold (business-ready). Skipping a tier = INCOMPLETE.
rule.rationale: Implicit tiers cause silent quality regressions downstream.

rule.statement: Every task MUST be idempotent — rerun produces identical output, no duplicates. Non-idempotent task = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Reruns are the most-common failure-recovery path; non-idempotent = corrupted gold.

rule.statement: Every stream / batch ingest MUST have a Dead-Letter Queue with a documented inspection cadence.
rule.rationale: Failed records silently dropped = silent quality regression.

rule.statement: Schema changes upstream MUST pass through a schema-registry / contract-test gate before silver-tier writes.
rule.rationale: Unvalidated schema drift breaks gold-tier consumers.

rule.statement: Every gold-tier mart MUST have data-quality tests (great_expectations / dbt tests) running per refresh.
rule.rationale: Untested gold tables propagate bad numbers to dashboards + ML.

rule.statement: Pipeline declares a latency SLA (e.g. p95 bronze→gold < 15min) and emits a metric per refresh.
rule.rationale: Latency drift becomes 'why is the dashboard 6 hours stale'.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
